{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\nconst { GraphDatabaseAdapter } = require('/coding/integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/dist/storage/graph-database-adapter.js');\nconst db = new GraphDatabaseAdapter('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ndb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const entities = await db.queryEntities({});\n  const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n  const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n  const withEnriched = entities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n  console.log('Total entities:', entities.length);\n  console.log('With 384-dim embeddings:', withEmb.length);\n  console.log('With role:', withRole.length);\n  console.log('With enrichedContext:', withEnriched.length);\n  if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n    const e = withEmb[0];\n    const emb = e.embedding;\n    const unique = new Set(emb.slice(0,20).map(v => v.toFixed(6))).size;\n    console.log('Sample:', e.name, 'emb[0:3]:', emb.slice(0,3), 'unique_in_20:', unique);\n  }\n  if (withRole.length > 0) {\n    console.log('Sample role:', withRole[0].name, '->', withRole[0].role);\n  }\n  await db.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check entities for operator fields mid-pipeline",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

  "description": "Read Graphology graph directly for operator fields",
  "timeout": 30000
}

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\nconst { GraphDatabaseAdapter } = require('/coding/integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/dist/storage/graph-database-adapter.js');\nconst db = new GraphDatabaseAdapter('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ndb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const entities = await db.queryEntities({});\n  const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n  const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n  const withEnriched = entities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n  console.log('Total entities:', entities.length);\n  console.log('With 384-dim embeddings:', withEmb.length);\n  console.log('With role:', withRole.length);\n  console.log('With enrichedContext:', withEnriched.length);\n  if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n    const e = withEmb[0];\n    const emb = e.embedding;\n    const unique = new Set(emb.slice(0,20).map(v => v.toFixed(6))).size;\n    console.log('Sample:', e.name, 'emb[0:3]:', emb.slice(0,3), 'unique_in_20:', unique);\n  }\n  if (withRole.length > 0) {\n    console.log('Sample role:', withRole[0].name, '->', withRole[0].role);\n  }\n  await db.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check entities for operator fields mid-pipeline",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\n// Bypass VKB API - read Graphology directly\nconst { GraphDatabaseService } = require('/coding/src/knowledge-management/GraphDatabaseService.js');\nconst gdb = new GraphDatabaseService('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ngdb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const graph = gdb.graph;\n  const nodes = graph.nodes();\n  let withEmb = 0, withRole = 0, withEnriched = 0;\n  let sample = null;\n  for (const nodeId of nodes.slice(0, 50)) {\n    const attrs = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodeId);\n    if (attrs.embedding && Array.isArray(attrs.embedding) && attrs.embedding.length === 384) {\n      withEmb++;\n      if (!sample) sample = { name: attrs.name || attrs.entity_name, emb: attrs.embedding.slice(0,3) };\n    }\n    if (attrs.role) withRole++;\n    if (attrs.enrichedContext) withEnriched++;\n  }\n  console.log('Checked 50 nodes. emb:', withEmb, 'role:', withRole, 'enriched:', withEnriched);\n  if (sample) console.log('Sample:', sample);\n  else {\n    // Show what fields the first node has\n    const first = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodes[0]);\n    console.log('First node keys:', Object.keys(first));\n  }\n  await gdb.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err.message));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Read Graphology graph directly for operator fields",
  "timeout": 30000
}

  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\nconst { GraphDatabaseService } = require(\"/coding/src/knowledge-management/GraphDatabaseService.js\");\nconst gdb = new GraphDatabaseService(\"/coding/.data/knowledge-graph\");\ngdb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const graph = gdb.graph;\n  const nodes = graph.nodes();\n  let withEmb = 0, withRole = 0;\n  let sampleKeys = null;\n  for (const nodeId of nodes.slice(0, 50)) {\n    const attrs = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodeId);\n    if (attrs.embedding && Array.isArray(attrs.embedding) && attrs.embedding.length === 384) withEmb++;\n    if (attrs.role) withRole++;\n    if (!sampleKeys) sampleKeys = Object.keys(attrs);\n  }\n  console.log(\"Checked 50 nodes. emb:\", withEmb, \"role:\", withRole);\n  console.log(\"First node keys:\", JSON.stringify(sampleKeys));\n  await gdb.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err.message));\n'",
  "description": "Read Graphology directly for operator fields",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

  "new_string": "    referencedProjectIds.forEach(projectId => {\n      const attributes = this.graph.getNodeAttributes(projectId);\n\n      // Apply team filter\n      if (team && attributes.team !== team) return;\n\n      results.push({\n        id: projectId,\n        entity_name: attributes.name,\n        entity_type: attributes.entityType || 'Unknown',\n        observations: attributes.observations || [],\n        extraction_type: attributes.extraction_type || null,\n        classification: attributes.classification || null,\n        confidence: attributes.confidence !== undefined ? attributes.confidence : 1.0,\n        source: attributes.source || 'manual',\n        team: attributes.team,\n        extracted_at: attributes.created_at || attributes.extracted_at,\n        last_modified: attributes.last_modified,\n        session_id: attributes.session_id || null,\n        embedding_id: attributes.embedding_id || null,\n        metadata: attributes.metadata || {},\n        // Hierarchy fields\n        parentEntityName: attributes.parentEntityName || null,\n        hierarchyLevel: attributes.hierarchyLevel != null ? attributes.hierarchyLevel : null,\n        isScaffoldNode: attributes.isScaffoldNode || false,\n        childEntityNames: attributes.childEntityNames || [],\n        // Operator-enriched fields\n        ...(attributes.embedding ? { embedding: attributes.embedding } : {}),\n        ...(attributes.role ? { role: attributes.role } : {}),\n        ...(attributes.enrichedContext ? { enrichedContext: attributes.enrichedContext } : {})\n      });"
}

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\nconst { GraphDatabaseAdapter } = require('/coding/integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/dist/storage/graph-database-adapter.js');\nconst db = new GraphDatabaseAdapter('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ndb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const entities = await db.queryEntities({});\n  const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n  const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n  const withEnriched = entities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n  console.log('Total entities:', entities.length);\n  console.log('With 384-dim embeddings:', withEmb.length);\n  console.log('With role:', withRole.length);\n  console.log('With enrichedContext:', withEnriched.length);\n  if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n    const e = withEmb[0];\n    const emb = e.embedding;\n    const unique = new Set(emb.slice(0,20).map(v => v.toFixed(6))).size;\n    console.log('Sample:', e.name, 'emb[0:3]:', emb.slice(0,3), 'unique_in_20:', unique);\n  }\n  if (withRole.length > 0) {\n    console.log('Sample role:', withRole[0].name, '->', withRole[0].role);\n  }\n  await db.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check entities for operator fields mid-pipeline",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\n// Bypass VKB API - read Graphology directly\nconst { GraphDatabaseService } = require('/coding/src/knowledge-management/GraphDatabaseService.js');\nconst gdb = new GraphDatabaseService('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ngdb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const graph = gdb.graph;\n  const nodes = graph.nodes();\n  let withEmb = 0, withRole = 0, withEnriched = 0;\n  let sample = null;\n  for (const nodeId of nodes.slice(0, 50)) {\n    const attrs = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodeId);\n    if (attrs.embedding && Array.isArray(attrs.embedding) && attrs.embedding.length === 384) {\n      withEmb++;\n      if (!sample) sample = { name: attrs.name || attrs.entity_name, emb: attrs.embedding.slice(0,3) };\n    }\n    if (attrs.role) withRole++;\n    if (attrs.enrichedContext) withEnriched++;\n  }\n  console.log('Checked 50 nodes. emb:', withEmb, 'role:', withRole, 'enriched:', withEnriched);\n  if (sample) console.log('Sample:', sample);\n  else {\n    // Show what fields the first node has\n    const first = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodes[0]);\n    console.log('First node keys:', Object.keys(first));\n  }\n  await gdb.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err.message));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Read Graphology graph directly for operator fields",
  "timeout": 30000
}

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Check VKB server graph directly\nconst vkbPath = \"/coding/lib/vkb-server/\";\n// The VKB server has its own GraphDatabaseService instance\n// Check if any entity was stored with embedding field by looking at the graph attributes\n// Find the VKB server process memory - we need to query through the API\n// Try a specific entity that would have been recently created\nconst http = require(\"http\");\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=200\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (chunk) => data += chunk);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(data);\n    const entities = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : parsed.entities || [];\n    const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding);\n    const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n    console.log(\"Total:\", entities.length, \"With embedding:\", withEmb.length, \"With role:\", withRole.length);\n    // Check last_modified to find recently stored entities\n    const recent = entities.filter(e => e.last_modified > \"2026-03-08T07:59\").slice(0, 3);\n    console.log(\"Recent (after current run start):\", recent.length);\n    for (const e of recent) {\n      console.log(\" \", e.entity_name, \"modified:\", e.last_modified, \"keys:\", Object.keys(e).join(\",\"));\n    }\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check VKB API for recent entities with operator fields",
  "timeout": 15000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

I'll address that after we verify the primary goal...

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\nconst { GraphDatabaseAdapter } = require('/coding/integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/dist/storage/graph-database-adapter.js');\nconst db = new GraphDatabaseAdapter('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ndb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const entities = await db.queryEntities({});\n  const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n  const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n  const withEnriched = entities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n  console.log('Total entities:', entities.length);\n  console.log('With 384-dim embeddings:', withEmb.length);\n  console.log('With role:', withRole.length);\n  console.log('With enrichedContext:', withEnriched.length);\n  if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n    const e = withEmb[0];\n    const emb = e.embedding;\n    const unique = new Set(emb.slice(0,20).map(v => v.toFixed(6))).size;\n    console.log('Sample:', e.name, 'emb[0:3]:', emb.slice(0,3), 'unique_in_20:', unique);\n  }\n  if (withRole.length > 0) {\n    console.log('Sample role:', withRole[0].name, '->', withRole[0].role);\n  }\n  await db.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check entities for operator fields mid-pipeline",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\n// Bypass VKB API - read Graphology directly\nconst { GraphDatabaseService } = require('/coding/src/knowledge-management/GraphDatabaseService.js');\nconst gdb = new GraphDatabaseService('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ngdb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const graph = gdb.graph;\n  const nodes = graph.nodes();\n  let withEmb = 0, withRole = 0, withEnriched = 0;\n  let sample = null;\n  for (const nodeId of nodes.slice(0, 50)) {\n    const attrs = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodeId);\n    if (attrs.embedding && Array.isArray(attrs.embedding) && attrs.embedding.length === 384) {\n      withEmb++;\n      if (!sample) sample = { name: attrs.name || attrs.entity_name, emb: attrs.embedding.slice(0,3) };\n    }\n    if (attrs.role) withRole++;\n    if (attrs.enrichedContext) withEnriched++;\n  }\n  console.log('Checked 50 nodes. emb:', withEmb, 'role:', withRole, 'enriched:', withEnriched);\n  if (sample) console.log('Sample:', sample);\n  else {\n    // Show what fields the first node has\n    const first = graph.getNodeAttributes(nodes[0]);\n    console.log('First node keys:', Object.keys(first));\n  }\n  await gdb.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err.message));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Read Graphology graph directly for operator fields",
  "timeout": 30000
}

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

I'll address that after we verify the primary goal...

I'll note it as a separate issue to fix. For now let's stay focused on verifying the embedding results once this run completes — that's the primary gate for Phase 10 sign-off.

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e \"\nconst { GraphDatabaseAdapter } = require('/coding/integrations/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/dist/storage/graph-database-adapter.js');\nconst db = new GraphDatabaseAdapter('/coding/.data/knowledge-graph');\ndb.initialize().then(async () => {\n  const entities = await db.queryEntities({});\n  const withEmb = entities.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n  const withRole = entities.filter(e => e.role);\n  const withEnriched = entities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n  console.log('Total entities:', entities.length);\n  console.log('With 384-dim embeddings:', withEmb.length);\n  console.log('With role:', withRole.length);\n  console.log('With enrichedContext:', withEnriched.length);\n  if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n    const e = withEmb[0];\n    const emb = e.embedding;\n    const unique = new Set(emb.slice(0,20).map(v => v.toFixed(6))).size;\n    console.log('Sample:', e.name, 'emb[0:3]:', emb.slice(0,3), 'unique_in_20:', unique);\n  }\n  if (withRole.length > 0) {\n    console.log('Sample role:', withRole[0].name, '->', withRole[0].role);\n  }\n  await db.close();\n}).catch(err => console.error(err));\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Check entities for operator fields mid-pipeline",
  "timeout": 30000
}
```

  "description": "Read Graphology graph directly for operator fields",

}

}

  "timeout": 10000
}

{
  "command": "docker exec coding-services node -e '\n// Use the running VKB server graph - query a node directly\nconst http = require(\"http\");\n// Get entities, then check one specifically via the advanced query\nconst options = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=3\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(options, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    // Pick a recently modified entity\n    const recent = ents.sort((a,b) => b.last_modified.localeCompare(a.last_modified))[0];\n    console.log(\"Most recent:\", recent.entity_name, \"modified:\", recent.last_modified);\n    // No raw query available - the VKB API is the bottleneck\n    // Need to check what queryEntities returns from the graph\n    console.log(\"Keys returned:\", Object.keys(recent).join(\", \"));\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n'",
  "description": "Check most recent entity from VKB API",
  "timeout": 10000
}
```

I'll address that after we verify the primary goal...

**User Message:** still unclear why Persistence has 3 sub-steps, but they are not shown and I cannot manually open them by clicking the blue circle (3) - unlike all other multi-step agents

**Assistant Response:** That's a dashboard UI bug. The Persist node shows "3" (its 3 sub-steps: per-wave persist for waves 1-3) but doesn't expand on click like Semantic, Insights, or KG-Ops do. I'll note it as a separate issue to fix. For now let's stay focused on verifying the embedding results once this run completes — that's the primary gate for Phase 10 sign-off.

{
  "command": "cat /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.data/workflow-progress.json | python3 -c \"import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'status={d.get(\\\"status\\\")} step={d.get(\\\"currentStep\\\")}')\" && docker exec coding-services node -e '\nconst http = require(\"http\");\nconst opts = { hostname: \"localhost\", port: 8080, path: \"/api/entities?team=coding&limit=200\", method: \"GET\" };\nconst req = http.request(opts, (res) => {\n  let data = \"\";\n  res.on(\"data\", (c) => data += c);\n  res.on(\"end\", () => {\n    const entities = JSON.parse(data);\n    const ents = Array.isArray(entities) ? entities : entities.entities || [];\n    const withEmb = ents.filter(e => e.embedding && Array.isArray(e.embedding) && e.embedding.length === 384);\n    const withRole = ents.filter(e => e.role);\n    const withEnriched = ents.filter(e => e.enrichedContext);\n    console.log(\"Total:\", ents.length, \"With embedding:\", withEmb.length, \"With role:\", withRole.length, \"With enrichedContext:\", withEnriched.length);\n    if (withEmb.length > 0) {\n      const e = withEmb[0];\n      console.log(\"Sample:\", e.entity_name, \"emb[0:3]:\", e.embedding.slice(0,3));\n    }\n  });\n});\nreq.end();\n' 2>&1",
  "description": "Check pipeline status and entity fields",
  "timeout": 15000
}
```

### Bash - 2026-03-08 08:22:51 UTC [09:22:51 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-08_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-08_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-08_0700-0800_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_1500-1600_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_1400-1500_c197ef.md
```


---

### Read - 2026-03-08 08:22:51 UTC [09:22:51 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-08_0900-1000_c197ef.md",
  "offset": 9000,
  "limit": 400
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
  9000→**Result:** ✅ Success
  9001→**Output:** ```

  9003→  1116→        if (!nameMatch) return;
  9004→  1117→      }
  9005→  1118→
  9006→  1119→      // Add to filtered insight nodes set (NOT System nodes)
  9007→  1120→      filteredInsightNodeIds.add(nodeId);
  9008→  1121→
  9009→  1122→      // Map to SQL-compatible format
  9010→  1123→      results.push({
  9011
...[truncated]
```

### Read - 2026-03-08 08:22:51 UTC [09:22:51 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Read
**Input:** ```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/2026-03-08_0900-1000_c197ef.md",
  "offset": 1,
  "limit": 50
}
```

- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

### Read - 2026-03-08 08:22:51 UTC [09:22:51 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

⏺ Context is at 90%. Let me be direct about what I've found and what needs to happen.

  The pipeline has too many choke points. I've fixed 5 layers but embeddings still aren't stored. The re-persist at line 320 calls
  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Additionally, the user wants ALL issues fixed now:
  1. Operator-enriched fields not reaching GraphDB (embedding/role/enrichedContext)
  2. KG-Ops only showing 3/6 sub-steps on dashboard
  3. Wave sub-steps (classify, persist) jumping on dashboard
  4. Persist node sub-steps not expandable on click

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

}

e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Merge (structure fusion)\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Structure Fusion' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50)); // Allow SSE broadcast to pick up operator step\n        try {\n          const merged = await kgOperators.structureMerge(\n            { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations },\n            accumulatedKG\n          );\n          log('[WaveController] Merge operator complete', 'info', { entities: merged.entities.length, relations: merged.relations.length });\n          currentEntities = merged.entities;\n          currentRelations = merged.relations;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Merge operator failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Re-attach ontology metadata that operators may have stripped\n        for (const entity of currentEntities) {\n          if (!(entity as any)._ontologyMetadata && ontologyMap.has(entity.name)) {\n            (entity as any)._ontologyMetadata = ontologyMap.get(entity.name);\n          }\n        }\n\n        // Re-persist refined entities back to the KG\n        log('[WaveController] Re-persisting operator-refined entities', 'info', { count: currentEntities.length });\n        try {\n          const refinedWaveResult: WaveResult = {\n            wave: 3,\n            agentOutputs: [{\n              entities: currentEntities,\n              relationships: currentRelations,\n              childManifest: [],\n              discovered: false,\n              durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n              parentId: '',\n              agentName: 'KGOperators',\n            }],\n            totalEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            manifestEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => !e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            discoveredEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n            success: true,\n          };\n          await this.persistWaveResult(refinedWaveResult);\n          log('[WaveController] Operator-refined entities persisted', 'info');\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Re-persist after operators failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }",
  "new_string": "        // --- Run each operator with full trace logging and SSE visibility ---\n        const operatorTimings: Record<string, number> = {};\n\n        // Conv\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Context Convolution' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          const convStart = Date.now();\n          const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n          operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n          const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? 0,\n            sampleEntity: sampleEmb?.name ?? 'none',\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Embed operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Dedup\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Deduplication' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const dedupStart = Date.now();\n          const dedupInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          const deduped = await kgOperators.deduplication(currentEntities, accumulatedKG);\n          operatorTimings.dedup = Date.now() - dedupStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Dedup complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: dedupInputCount, outputEntities: deduped.entities.length,\n            merged: deduped.merged, durationMs: operatorTimings.dedup,\n            mergeLog: deduped.mergeLog.slice(0, 5), // First 5 merges for trace\n          });\n          currentEntities = deduped.entities;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Dedup operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Pred\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Edge Prediction' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const predStart = Date.now();\n          const relsBefore = currentRelations.length;\n          const predicted = await kgOperators.edgePrediction(currentEntities, { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations });\n          operatorTimings.pred = Date.now() - predStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Pred complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: currentEntities.length, relationsBefore: relsBefore,\n            predictedEdges: predicted.edges.length, durationMs: operatorTimings.pred,\n            topScores: predicted.scores.slice(0, 3).map(s => ({ from: s.from, to: s.to, score: s.score.toFixed(3) })),\n          });\n          currentRelations = [...currentRelations, ...predicted.edges];\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Pred operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Merge (structure fusion)\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Structure Fusion' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const mergeStart = Date.now();\n          const merged = await kgOperators.structureMerge(\n            { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations },\n            accumulatedKG\n          );\n          operatorTimings.merge = Date.now() - mergeStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Merge complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: currentEntities.length, inputRelations: currentRelations.length,\n            outputEntities: merged.entities.length, outputRelations: merged.relations.length,\n            added: merged.added, updated: merged.updated, durationMs: operatorTimings.merge,\n          });\n          currentEntities = merged.entities;\n          currentRelations = merged.relations;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Merge operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Log operator pipeline summary\n        const totalOperatorTime = Object.values(operatorTimings).reduce((s, t) => s + t, 0);\n        const embCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.embedding && e.embedding.length > 0).length;\n        const roleCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.role).length;\n        const ctxCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n        log('[WaveController] === OPERATOR PIPELINE SUMMARY ===', 'info', {\n          totalEntities: currentEntities.length, totalRelations: currentRelations.length,\n          withEmbeddings: embCount, withRole: roleCount, withEnrichedContext: ctxCount,\n          totalDurationMs: totalOperatorTime, operatorTimings,\n        });\n\n        // Re-attach ontology metadata that operators may have stripped\n        for (const entity of currentEntities) {\n          if (!(entity as any)._ontologyMetadata && ontologyMap.has(entity.name)) {\n            (entity as write embedding/role/enrichedContext\n        // directly to existing graph nodes via graphDB.mergeNodeAttributes()\n        log('[WaveController] Direct graph write: merging operator-enriched fields', 'info', {\n          entitiesWithEmbedding: embCount, entitiesWithRole: roleCount, entitiesWithContext: ctxCount,\n        });\n        let directWriteSuccess = 0;\n        let directWriteFail = 0;\n        for (const entity of currentEntities) {\n          const enrichedAttrs: Record<string, any> = {};\n          if (entity.embedding && entity.embedding.length > 0) enrichedAttrs.embedding = entity.embedding;\n          if (entity.role) enrichedAttrs.role = entity.role;\n          if (entity.enrichedContext) enrichedAttrs.enrichedContext = entity.enrichedContext;\n\n          if (Object.keys(enrichedAttrs).length > 0) {\n            try {\n              // Write directly to graph node — no pipeline, no transformation\n              const nodeId = `${this.team}:${entity.name}`;\n              await this.graphDB.mergeAttributes(nodeId, enrichedAttrs);\n              directWriteSuccess++;\n            } catch (e) {\n              directWriteFail++;\n              // Node might not exist yet — will be created by re-persist below\n              log('[WaveController] Direct graph write failed for entity (will try via persist)', 'debug', {\n                entity: entity.name, error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e),\n              });\n            }\n          }\n        }\n        log('[WaveController] Direct graph write complete', 'info', {\n          success: directWriteSuccess, failed: directWriteFail,\n        });\n\n        // Re-persist refined entities back to the KG (ensures new entities are created)\n        log('[WaveController] Re-persisting operator-refined entities via pipeline', 'info', { count: currentEntities.length });\n        try {\n          const refinedWaveResult: WaveResult = {\n            wave: 3,\n            agentOutputs: [{\n              entities: currentEntities,\n              relationships: currentRelations,\n              childManifest: [],\n              discovered: false,\n              durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n              parentId: '',\n              agentName: 'KGOperators',\n            }],\n            totalEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            manifestEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => !e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            discoveredEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n            success: true,\n          };\n          await this.persistWaveResult(refinedWaveResult);\n          log('[WaveController] Operator-refined entities persisted via pipeline', 'info');\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Re-persist after operators failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/storage/graph-database-adapter.ts",
  "old_string": "  /**\n   * Delete an entity from the graph database\n   * Used by ContentValidationAgent to remove stale entities\n   * Supports both VKB API and direct GraphDatabaseService access\n   */",
  "new_string": "  /**\n   * Merge attributes directly into an existing graph node.\n   * Used for operator-enriched fields (embedding, role, enrichedContext)\n   * that need to bypass the full persist pipeline.\n   */\n  async mergeAttributes(nodeId: string, attributes: Record<string, any>): Promise<void> {\n    if (!this.isInitialized) {\n      throw new Error('GraphDatabaseAdapter not initialized. Call initialize() first.');\n    }\n\n    try {\n      if (this.useApi && this.apiClient) {\n        // Use VKB API - PATCH entity with partial update\n        const entityName = nodeId.includes(':') ? nodeId.split(':')[1] : nodeId;\n        await this.apiClient.updateEntity(entityName, attributes, { team: this.team });\n        log('Attributes merged via VKB API', 'debug', { nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes) });\n      } else if (this.graphDB) {\n        // Direct access — use Graphology's mergeNodeAttributes\n        const graph = this.graphDB.getGraph?.() ?? (this.graphDB as any).graph;\n        if (graph && graph.hasNode(nodeId)) {\n          graph.mergeNodeAttributes(nodeId, attributes);\n          log('Attributes merged via direct graph access', 'debug', { nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes) });\n        } else {\n          throw new Error(`Node ${nodeId} not found in graph`);\n        }\n      } else {\n        throw new Error('No database access available');\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      log('Failed to merge attributes', 'warning', {\n        nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes),\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Delete an entity from the graph database\n   * Used by ContentValidationAgent to remove stale entities\n   * Supports both VKB API and direct GraphDatabaseService access\n   */"
}

  "new_string": "  // Write to stderr for debugging (visible in terminal)\n  // Use minimal formatting to reduce blocking\n  console.error(`[${entry.timestamp}] ${level.toUpperCase()}: ${message}`);\n  if (data && (level === 'error' || level === 'warning' || message.includes('TRACE') || message.includes('SUMMARY'))) {\n    // Log data for errors, warnings, and trace/summary entries\n    console.error(`  Data: ${JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}`);\n  }"
}

  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Additionally, the user wants ALL issues fixed now:
  1. Operator-enriched fields not reaching GraphDB (embedding/role/enrichedContext)
  2. KG-Ops only showing 3/6 sub-steps on dashboard
  3. Wave sub-steps (classify, persist) jumping on dashboard
  4. Persist node sub-steps not expandable on click

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Dedup\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Deduplication' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50)); // Allow SSE broadcast to pick up operator step\n        try {\n          const deduped = await kgOperators.deduplication(currentEntities, accumulatedKG);\n          log('[WaveController] Dedup operator complete', 'info', { before: currentEntities.length, after: deduped.entities.length, merged: deduped.merged });\n          currentEntities = deduped.entities;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Dedup operator failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Pred\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Edge Prediction' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50)); // Allow SSE broadcast to pick up operator step\n        try {\n          const predicted = await kgOperators.edgePrediction(currentEntities, { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations });\n          log('[WaveController] Pred operator complete', 'info', { predictedEdges: predicted.edges.length });\n          currentRelations = [...currentRelations, ...predicted.edges];\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Pred operator failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Merge (structure fusion)\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Structure Fusion' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 50)); // Allow SSE broadcast to pick up operator step\n        try {\n          const merged = await kgOperators.structureMerge(\n            { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations },\n            accumulatedKG\n          );\n          log('[WaveController] Merge operator complete', 'info', { entities: merged.entities.length, relations: merged.relations.length });\n          currentEntities = merged.entities;\n          currentRelations = merged.relations;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Merge operator failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }\n\n        // Re-attach ontology metadata that operators may have stripped\n        for (const entity of currentEntities) {\n          if (!(entity as any)._ontologyMetadata && ontologyMap.has(entity.name)) {\n            (entity as any)._ontologyMetadata = ontologyMap.get(entity.name);\n          }\n        }\n\n        // Re-persist refined entities back to the KG\n        log('[WaveController] Re-persisting operator-refined entities', 'info', { count: currentEntities.length });\n        try {\n          const refinedWaveResult: WaveResult = {\n            wave: 3,\n            agentOutputs: [{\n              entities: currentEntities,\n              relationships: currentRelations,\n              childManifest: [],\n              discovered: false,\n              durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n              parentId: '',\n              agentName: 'KGOperators',\n            }],\n            totalEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            manifestEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => !e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            discoveredEntities: currentEntities.filter(e => e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n            success: true,\n          };\n          await this.persistWaveResult(refinedWaveResult);\n          log('[WaveController] Operator-refined entities persisted', 'info');\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Re-persist after operators failed (non-fatal)', 'warning', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e) }); }",
  "new_string": "        // --- Run each operator with full trace logging and SSE visibility ---\n        const operatorTimings: Record<string, number> = {};\n\n        // Conv\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Context Convolution' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100)); // Allow SSE broadcast\n        try {\n          const convStart = Date.now();\n          const convInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.contextConvolution(currentEntities, batchContext);\n          operatorTimings.conv = Date.now() - convStart;\n          const withContext = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Conv complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: convInputCount, outputEntities: currentEntities.length,\n            withEnrichedContext: withContext, durationMs: operatorTimings.conv,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Conv operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Aggr\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Entity Aggregation' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const aggrStart = Date.now();\n          const aggrInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          const aggr = await kgOperators.entityAggregation(currentEntities);\n          currentEntities = [...aggr.core, ...aggr.nonCore];\n          operatorTimings.aggr = Date.now() - aggrStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Aggr complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: aggrInputCount, core: aggr.core.length, nonCore: aggr.nonCore.length,\n            durationMs: operatorTimings.aggr,\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Aggr operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Embed\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Node Embedding' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const embedStart = Date.now();\n          const embedInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          const beforeEmbCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.embedding && e.embedding.length > 0).length;\n          currentEntities = await kgOperators.nodeEmbedding(currentEntities);\n          operatorTimings.embed = Date.now() - embedStart;\n          const afterEmbCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.embedding && e.embedding.length > 0).length;\n          const sampleEmb = currentEntities.find(e => e.embedding && e.embedding.length > 0);\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Embed complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: embedInputCount, embeddingsBefore: beforeEmbCount, embeddingsAfter: afterEmbCount,\n            newEmbeddings: afterEmbCount - beforeEmbCount, durationMs: operatorTimings.embed,\n            sampleDimensions: sampleEmb?.embedding?.length ?? 0,\n            sampleEntity: sampleEmb?.name ?? 'none',\n          });\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Embed operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Dedup\n        this.updateProgress({ currentWave: 3, totalWaves: 4, subPhase: 'operators', message: 'KG Operator: Deduplication' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const dedupStart = Date.now();\n          const dedupInputCount = currentEntities.length;\n          const deduped = await kgOperators.deduplication(currentEntities, accumulatedKG);\n          operatorTimings.dedup = Date.now() - dedupStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Dedup complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: dedupInputCount, outputEntities: deduped.entities.length,\n            merged: deduped.merged, durationMs: operatorTimings.dedup,\n            mergeLog: deduped.mergeLog.slice(0, 5), // First 5 merges for trace\n          });\n          currentEntities = deduped.entities;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Dedup operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error Operator: Edge Prediction' });\n        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const predStart = Date.now();\n          const relsBefore = currentRelations.length;\n durationMs: operatorTimings.pred,\n            topScores: predicted.scores.slice(0, 3).map(s => ({ from: s.from, to: s.to, score: s.score.toFixed(3) })),\n          });\n          currentRelations = [...currentRelations, ...predicted.edges];\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Pred operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ?    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));\n        try {\n          const mergeStart = Date.now();\n          const merged = await kgOperators.structureMerge(\n            { entities: currentEntities, relations: currentRelations },\n            accumulatedKG\n          );\n          operatorTimings.merge = Date.now() - mergeStart;\n          log('[WaveController] OPERATOR TRACE: Merge complete', 'info', {\n            inputEntities: currentEntities.length, inputRelations: currentRelations.length,\n            outputEntities: merged.entities.length, outputRelations: merged.relations.length,\n            added: merged.added, updated: merged.updated, durationMs: operatorTimings.merge,\n          });\n          currentEntities = merged.entities;\n          currentRelations = merged.relations;\n        } catch (e) { log('[WaveController] Merge operator FAILED', 'error', { error: e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e), stack: e instanceof Error ? e.stack : undefined }); }\n\n        // Log operator pipeline summary\n        const totalOperatorTime = Object.values(operatorTimings).reduce((s, t) => s + t, 0);\n        const embCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.embedding && e.embedding.length > 0).length;\n        const roleCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.role).length;\n        const ctxCount = currentEntities.filter(e => e.enrichedContext).length;\n        log('[WaveController] === OPERATOR PIPELINE SUMMARY ===', 'info', {\n          totalEntities: currentEntities.length, totalRelations: currentRelations.length,\n metadata that operators may have stripped\n        for (const entity of currentEntities) {\n          if (!(entity as any)._ontologyMetadata && ontologyMap.has(entity.name)) {\n            (entity as any)._ontologyMetadata = ontologyMap.get(entity.name);\n          }\n        }\n\n        // === DIRECT GRAPH WRITE for operator-enriched fields ===\n        // Bypass the 7-layer persist pipeline — write embedding/role/enrichedContext\n        // directly to existing graph nodes via graphDB.mergeNodeAttributes()\n        log('[WaveController] Direct graph write: merging operator-enriched enrichedAttrs.embedding = entity.embedding;\n          if (entity.role) enrichedAttrs.role = entity.role;\n          if (entity.enrichedContext) enrichedAttrs.enrichedContext = entity.enrichedContext;\n\n          if (Object.keys(enrichedAttrs).length > 0) {\n            try {\n              // Write directly to graph node — no pipeline, no transformation\n              const nodeId = `${this.team}:${entity.name}`;\n              await this.graphDB.mergeAttributes(nodeId, enrichedAttrs);\n              directWriteSuccess++;\n            } catch (e) {\n              directWriteFail++;\n              // Node might not exist yet — will be created by re-persist below\n              log('[WaveController] Direct graph write failed for entity (will try via persist)', e.id?.startsWith('discovered:')).length,\n            durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n            success: true,\n          };\n          await this.persistWaveResult(refinedWaveResult);\n   The pipeline has too many choke points. I've fixed 5 layers but embeddings still aren't stored. The re-persist at line 320 calls
  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Additionally, the user wants ALL issues fixed now:
  1. Operator-enriched fields not reaching GraphDB (embedding/role/enrichedContext)
  2. KG-Ops only showing 3/6 sub-steps on dashboard
  3. Wave sub-steps (classify, persist) jumping on dashboard
  4. Persist node sub-steps not expandable on click

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "<AWS_SECRET_REDACTED>ns/mcp-server-semantic-analysis/src/storage/graph-database-adapter.ts",
  "old_string": "  /**\n   * Delete an entity from the graph database\n   * Used by ContentValidationAgent to remove stale entities\n   * Supports both VKB API and direct GraphDatabaseService access\n   */",
  "new_string": "  /**\n   * Merge attributes directly into an existing graph node.\n   * Used for operator-enriched fields (embedding, role, enrichedContext)\n   * that need to bypass the full persist pipeline.\n   */\n  async mergeAttributes(nodeId: string, attributes: Record<string, any>): Promise<void> {\n    if (!this.isInitialized) {\n      throw new Error('GraphDatabaseAdapter not initialized. Call initialize() first.');\n    }\n\n    try {\n      if (this.useApi && this.apiClient) {\n        // Use VKB API - PATCH entity with partial update\n        const entityName = nodeId.includes(':') ? nodeId.split(':')[1] : nodeId;\n        await this.apiClient.updateEntity(entityName, attributes, { team: this.team });\n        log('Attributes merged via VKB API', 'debug', { nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes) });\n      } else if (this.graphDB) {\n        // Direct access — use Graphology's mergeNodeAttributes\n        const graph = this.graphDB.getGraph?.() ?? (this.graphDB as any).graph;\n        if (graph && graph.hasNode(nodeId)) {\n          graph.mergeNodeAttributes(nodeId, attributes);\n          log('Attributes merged via direct graph access', 'debug', { nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes) });\n        } else {\n          throw new Error(`Node ${nodeId} not found in graph`);\n        }\n      } else {\n        throw new Error('No database access available');\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      log('Failed to merge attributes', 'warning', {\n        nodeId, keys: Object.keys(attributes),\n        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),\n      });\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Delete an entity from the graph database\n   * Used by ContentValidationAgent to remove stale entities\n   * Supports both VKB API and direct GraphDatabaseService access\n   */"
}

⏺ Still 0 embeddings. The fields aren't making it through despite all the fixes. The problem might be that operators run but the re-persist after
   operators isn't actually calling the VKB API path. Let me check:

⏺ Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Context is at 90%. Let me be direct about what I've found and what needs to happen.

  The pipeline has too many choke points. I've fixed 5 layers but embeddings still aren't stored. The re-persist at line 320 calls
  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Additionally, the user wants ALL issues fixed now:
  1. Operator-enriched fields not reaching GraphDB (embedding/role/enrichedContext)
  2. KG-Ops only showing 3/6 sub-steps on dashboard
  3. Wave sub-steps (classify, persist) jumping on dashboard
  4. Persist node sub-steps not expandable on click

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

  Want me to /clear and tackle all 4 issues in a fresh context?

}

⏺ Context is at 90%. Let me be direct about what I've found and what needs to happen.

  The pipeline has too many choke points. I've fixed 5 layers but embeddings still aren't stored. The re-persist at line 320 calls
  persistWaveResult → mapEntityToSharedMemory → persistEntities (persistence-agent) → VKB API → UKBDatabaseWriter → KnowledgeQueryService →
  GraphDatabaseService.storeEntity. That's 7 layers deep, and any one can silently drop fields.

  Additionally, the user wants ALL issues fixed now:
  1. Operator-enriched fields not reaching GraphDB (embedding/role/enrichedContext)
  2. KG-Ops only showing 3/6 sub-steps on dashboard
  3. Wave sub-steps (classify, persist) jumping on dashboard
  4. Persist node sub-steps not expandable on click

  Context is too low to debug all of these. I recommend:

  /clear and start fresh with a focused approach — instead of chasing field propagation through 7 layers, use a direct GraphDB update after
  operators complete. Skip the full persist pipeline entirely and write embedding/role/enrichedContext directly to existing graph nodes via
  graphDB.storeEntity() or graph.mergeNodeAttributes(). This is architecturally cleaner than patching 7 layers.

  Want me to /clear and tackle all 4 issues in a fresh context?

## Prompt Set (ps_1772958171359)

**Time:** 2026-03-08T08:22:51.359Z
**Duration:** 0ms
**Tool Calls:** 6

### Bash - 2026-03-08 08:22:51 UTC [09:22:51 CEST]

**User Request:** # Session Logs (/sl) - Session Continuity Command

Load and summarize recent Live Session Logs (LSL) to provide continuity from previous work sessions.

## Instructions

**Goal**: Load recent LSL files and produce a summary to establish context continuity.

### Step 1: Determine Current Project

1. Get the current working directory
2. Identify the project name (the directory name containing `.specstory/history`)
3. Note if this IS the `coding` project or a DIFFERENT project

### Step 2: Load LSL Files from Current Project

1. List files in `.specstory/history/` sorted by modification time (most recent first)
2. Load the **most recent** LSL file
3. Check file size/content length:
   - If file is **short** (less than 50KB or fewer than 500 lines of actual content), also load 1-2 previous files
   - "Short" means the session was brief and may not have enough context
4. Note the **timestamp range** from the oldest loaded file's filename (format: `YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM`)

### Step 3: Load Coding Project LSL Files (Cross-Project Context)

**Only if current project is NOT `coding`:**

1. Check `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/` for LSL files
2. Find files that fall within or overlap the timestamp range from Step 2
3. Load the most recent coding LSL file from that time range
4. If that file is short, also load 1-2 previous files from coding

**If current project IS `coding`:** Skip this step (already handled in Step 2)

### Step 4: Produce Summary

Create a concise summary covering:

1. **Time Range**: When these sessions occurred
2. **Projects Involved**: Which projects had activity
3. **Key Topics/Tasks**: Main things worked on in each session
4. **Current State**: What was left in progress or recently completed
5. **Suggested Next Steps**: Any obvious continuity items

Format the summary as:

```
## Session Continuity Summary

**Time Range**: [oldest file date] to [newest file date]
**Projects**: [list of projects with activity]

### Recent Work:
- [Project A]: [Brief description of main tasks]
- [Project B]: [Brief description of main tasks]

### Current State:
[What was in progress or just completed]

### Suggested Continuity:
[Any obvious next steps or items to pick up]
```

## File Selection Logic

- **Primary criterion**: Recency (most recent first)
- **Secondary criterion**: Length (short files trigger loading more files)
- **Tertiary criterion**: Cross-project relevance (coding project files during same timeframe)

## Size Thresholds

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection

---

**Begin by identifying the current project and loading the most recent LSL file.**

**Tool:** Bash
**Input:** ```json
{
  "command": "ls -t /Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/ | head -5",
  "description": "List most recent LSL files"
}
```

**Result:** ✅ Success
**Output:** ```
2026-03-08_0900-1000_c197ef.md
2026-03-08_0800-0900_c197ef.md
2026-03-08_0700-0800_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_1500-1600_c197ef.md
2026-03-07_1400-1500_c197ef.md
```

- **Short file**: < 50KB or < 500 lines → load additional files
- **Medium file**: 50KB-200KB → sufficient context alone
- **Large file**: > 200KB → definitely sufficient, may want to focus on recent sections

## Path Constants

- Current project LSL: `.specstory/history/`
- Coding project LSL: `/Users/<USER_ID_REDACTED>/Agentic/coding/.specstory/history/`

## User Arguments

If user provides ``:
- Number (e.g., "3"): Load exactly that many recent files per project
- "all": Load all files from today
- Empty/default: Use automatic length-based detection
